_meta.source: CEV Informe Final 'Hay Futuro Si Hay Verdad' (2022) — esp. Tomo 'No matarás' (history) + 'Hasta la guerra tiene límites' (violations & collective responsibility)
_meta.responsibility_kind: COLLECTIVE (ethical-political), not individual-criminal (that is the JEP's role). Terminology: State/paramilitary acts = 'violaciones de derechos humanos'; guerrilla acts = 'abusos / infracciones al DIH'.
_meta.lethal_ordering: Most non-combatant dead were killed first by paramilitaries, then guerrillas, then State forces.

actors.name: Paramilitaries / Autodefensas (AUC)
actors.origin: Civilian-arming decrees (Decreto 3398/1965 → Ley 48/1968) → MAS (1981, 'hito fundacional') → Puerto Boyacá model → Casa Castaño / ACCU (1995) → Convivir (1995) → AUC (1997, Carlos Castaño) → demobilized via Justicia y Paz (2005), recycled into successor groups (Clan del Golfo/AGC, Rastrojos)
actors.key_finding: Direct State responsibility for the origin, training, strengthening and expansion of paramilitarism; joint action 'at least until 2005'. 'El trabajo sucio que ellos no podían hacer legalmente.'

actors.name: FARC-EP (Fuerzas Armadas Revolucionarias de Colombia - Ejército del Pueblo)
actors.origin: Communist autodefensas campesinas of southern Tolima (Marquetalia) → 1964 attack on Marquetalia (founding myth) → formally constituted 1966 → militarized & narco-financed via Conferencias VII (1982) / VIII (1993) → military peak late 1990s → pushed back after 2002 → signed the 2016 Final Peace Accord
actors.key_finding: Recognized kidnapping (JEP Caso 01) as war crimes and crimes against humanity. Recruitment was 'naturalized and intentional' — comisiones de reclutamiento from the 1982 conference.

actors.name: State / Fuerza Pública (Ejército, Policía, DAS intelligence)
actors.origin: Built 'for war' under Cold War 'enemigo interno' doctrine (Yarborough mission 1962, Escuela de las Américas); the 'cuarto poder' delegated public order from the Frente Nacional onward
principal_responsibility: 'falsos positivos' / extrajudicial executions (≥6,402, 2002-2008) — a crime of State
actors.key_finding: 'A mayor poder, mayor responsabilidad' — as guarantor of rights the State bears the heaviest duty. Responsible by action, omission, and tolerance/complicity (CorteIDH Velásquez Rodríguez, Mapiripán).

actors.name: ELN (Ejército de Liberación Nacional)
actors.origin: Founded 1964 (toma de Simacota 1965); built by students/workers, joined by priest Camilo Torres; nearly destroyed by Operación Anorí (1973); rebuilt on kidnapping & oil-pipeline rents (Caño Limón-Coveñas); NEVER demobilized — persists
actors.key_finding: A negotiated, definitive exit with the ELN is a core CEV recommendation (recovering 2017-2018 advances).

actors.name: EPL & M-19 (demobilized insurgencies)
actors.origin: EPL founded 1967 (maoist PC-ML); M-19 born from the fraudulent 1970 election. Both largely demobilized 1990-91 into the 1991 Constituent Assembly
actors.key_finding: Their demobilization fed the democratizing leap of the 1991 Constitution; EPL demobilized (Esperanza, Paz y Libertad) were then massacred.

actors.name: Narcotrafficking & terceros civiles (economic/political actors)
actors.origin: Bonanza marimbera → Medellín Cartel (Escobar) & Cali Cartel → Norte del Valle cartel; plus economic/political elites and businesses
principal_responsibility: war financing of all armed armies
actors.key_finding: A database of 258 economic actors presumed involved; 439 business actors sanctioned in 35 Justicia y Paz sentences. 'Una guerra donde no hay finanzas no es guerra.' The war on drugs itself is a persistence factor.
